title: Fix: thermal-lock password not zeroed
prompt:
SECURITY FIX — thermal-lock password never zeroed from memory.

Files to edit:
- crates/thermal-lock/Cargo.toml: add `zeroize = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }` to [dependencies]
- crates/thermal-lock/src/auth.rs:
  1. Add `use zeroize::Zeroizing;` at top
  2. Change `authenticate(username: &str, password: &str)` parameter to accept `password: &Zeroizing<String>` (or keep &str but wrap internally). The real fix is that `pass_c: CString` is never zeroed. After `pam_end()`, explicitly zero the CString bytes: before `pass_c` drops, call `unsafe { std::ptr::write_bytes(pass_c.as_ptr() as *mut u8, 0, pass_c.as_bytes().len()); }`
  3. The malloc'd PAM copy in `pam_conv_callback` is already freed by PAM — no change needed there.
- crates/thermal-lock/src/main.rs:
  1. Change `AuthState.password: String` to `password: Zeroizing<String>` (line ~297)
  2. In `AuthState::masked()` at line ~307, `self.password.chars()` still works since Zeroizing<String> derefs to String
  3. At line ~780-787, the `auth_snapshot` clones `app.auth.password.clone()` — change `AuthState.password` field in the snapshot to `String` (just use `auth.masked()` for rendering instead of cloning the real password — the snapshot only needs the masked string for display). Remove the real password from `AuthState` snapshot entirely; pass `masked_str: String` as a separate field.

Success criteria: `cargo check -p thermal-lock` passes with no errors.
notes: Done: added zeroize crate, changed AuthState.password to Zeroizing<String>, zero pass_c after pam_end, snapshot uses masked string not real password.

title: Fix: thermal-lock wrong Wayland surface pointer
prompt:
BUG FIX — wl_surface.id().as_ptr() returns an ObjectId internal pointer, not the wl_proxy* that wgpu/raw-window-handle expects via WaylandWindowHandle.

In wayland-client 0.31 with the `system` feature (already set in workspace Cargo.toml), ObjectId::as_ptr() IS the wl_proxy ptr. The current code is using `as *mut _` which is an unconstrained cast — the type annotation must explicitly be `*mut std::ffi::c_void` to be correct.

Fix both files:

1. crates/thermal-lock/src/main.rs line ~835:
   OLD: `let raw_surface_ptr = lock_surface.wl_surface().id().as_ptr() as *mut _;`
   NEW: `let raw_surface_ptr: *mut std::ffi::c_void = lock_surface.wl_surface().id().as_ptr().cast();`

2. crates/thermal-launch/src/main.rs line ~118:
   OLD: `NonNull::new(layer.wl_surface().id().as_ptr() as *mut _)`
   NEW: `NonNull::new(layer.wl_surface().id().as_ptr().cast::<std::ffi::c_void>())`

For thermal-notify (if it has a surface): apply the same pattern wherever `wl_surface().id().as_ptr()` is cast for use with wgpu.

Verify the `wayland-client` workspace dep has `features = ["system"]` — it does (Cargo.toml line: `wayland-client = { version = "0.31", features = ["system"] }`).

Success criteria: `cargo check -p thermal-lock -p thermal-launch` passes with no type errors on the surface pointer casts.
notes: Done: thermal-lock uses .cast() with explicit *mut c_void annotation; thermal-launch uses .cast::<std::ffi::c_void>(). Both cargo check clean.

title: Fix: thermal-lock missing pam_acct_mgmt
notes: Done: pam_acct_mgmt added to FFI extern block and called after pam_authenticate. Rate limiting added: failed_attempts counter + lockout_until: Option<Instant>, 2s lockout after 3 failures.

title: Fix: thermal-conductor poll() capture fallback
notes: Fixed: converted old_iter sequential traversal to old_indexed[i].take() for correct per-pane fallback on capture error.

title: Fix: thermal-conductor state detector Complete timeout
notes: Fixed: Complete timeout now checks new_state == Complete before returning Idle, preventing newly-computed non-Complete states from being discarded.

title: Fix: thermal-conductor audio thread leak
notes: Fixed: replaced stream_handle.play_raw() + hardcoded 2s sleep with Sink::sleep_until_end(), which blocks only for actual audio duration.

title: Fix: thermal-notify wire renderer to D-Bus
notes: DONE. Switched NotificationQueue from tokio::sync::Mutex to std::sync::Mutex so spawn_blocking render loop can lock without .await. Wired render loop in main.rs: creates NotifySurface + NotificationRenderer, drains queue into NotificationStack, calls renderer.render() per visible notification at ~60fps. Arc<Device>/Arc<Queue> exposed from NotifySurface.

title: Fix: state_detector error pattern too broad
notes: Fixed: replaced clean.contains(pattern) substring matching with contains_whole_word() helper using byte-boundary checks. No regex dependency added.

title: Fix: thermal-conductor dbus mutex poison risk
prompt:
BUG FIX — All D-Bus methods in ConductorInterface call `.lock().unwrap()` which will panic if a previous lock holder panicked, poisoning the mutex.

File: crates/thermal-conductor/src/dbus.rs

Replace every `.lock().unwrap()` with `.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())`.

Affected locations (all methods in the #[interface] impl block):
- Line ~32: `self.panes.lock().unwrap()` in create_pane()
- Line ~47: `self.active_pane.lock().unwrap()` in focus_pane()
- Line ~52: `self.layout.lock().unwrap()` in set_layout()
- Line ~67: `self.panes.lock().unwrap()` in panes() property
- Line ~72: `self.active_pane.lock().unwrap()` in active_pane() property
- Line ~77: `self.layout.lock().unwrap()` in layout() property

For each, change:
```rust
self.panes.lock().unwrap()
```
to:
```rust
self.panes.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
```

(Same pattern for active_pane and layout locks.)

Success criteria: `cargo check -p thermal-conductor` passes. No `.unwrap()` calls remain on mutex locks in dbus.rs.
notes: Fixed: replaced all .lock().unwrap() calls in dbus.rs with .lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) for poison recovery.

title: Fix: thermal-conductor Escape->Quit binding
prompt:
BUG FIX — InputHandler::handle() at line 55 maps Escape to InputAction::Quit. Escape is used constantly in terminal apps (vim normal mode, readline Ctrl-C fallback, etc.) so this would accidentally quit thermal-conductor while editing in a pane.

File: crates/thermal-conductor/src/input.rs

Change the Escape binding to Ctrl-Q:

OLD (line ~55):
```rust
Key::Named(NamedKey::Escape) => Some(InputAction::Quit),
```

NEW:
```rust
// Ctrl-Q quits thermal-conductor (Escape is reserved for terminal apps)
Key::Character(c) if c.as_str() == "q" || c.as_str() == "Q" => {
    // Check modifiers — but InputHandler doesn't track modifiers currently.
    // For now map Ctrl-Q by checking the character is 'q' with ctrl.
    // winit sends Ctrl-Q as Key::Character("\x11") (ASCII 17)
    None // fall through to Character handler below
}
```

Actually, winit reports Ctrl-Q as the character `\x11` (raw control char). The correct fix:

OLD:
```rust
Key::Named(NamedKey::Escape) => Some(InputAction::Quit),
```

NEW:
```rust
// Note: Escape is NOT quit — it's used by terminal apps.
// Ctrl-Q (\x11) quits thermal-conductor.
Key::Character(c) if c.as_str() == "\x11" => Some(InputAction::Quit),
```

And remove the `Key::Named(NamedKey::Escape) => Some(InputAction::Quit),` line entirely. The Escape key should fall through to the catch-all `_ => None`.

Success criteria: `cargo check -p thermal-conductor` passes.
notes: Fixed: removed Escape => Quit binding, replaced with Key::Character("\x11") => Quit so Ctrl-Q quits and Escape passes through to terminal apps.

title: Fix: thermal-conductor renderer hardcoded BG color
notes: Fixed: added srgb_to_linear() helper (IEC 61966-2-1 curve), replaced hardcoded color literals with ThermalPalette::BG values passed through linearization.

title: Fix: thermal-core WgpuContext async->sync
notes: Converted WgpuContext::new() from async fn to sync fn using pollster::block_on for both request_adapter and request_device calls. Added pollster = { workspace = true } to Cargo.toml. No callers found with .await — no call sites to update. cargo check -p thermal-core passes.

title: Fix: thermal-core remove unused deps
notes: Removed serde_json (unused in thermal-core src) and cosmic-text (transitive via glyphon) from Cargo.toml. Added pollster dep (needed for task 12). cargo check -p thermal-core passes.

title: Fix: thermal-core AgentState missing Hash derive
prompt:
CLEANUP — AgentState in crates/thermal-core/src/state.rs is `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]` (line 6) but does not derive `Hash`, preventing it from being used as HashMap/HashSet keys.

File: crates/thermal-core/src/state.rs

OLD (line 6):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum AgentState {
```

NEW:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum AgentState {
```

That's the entire fix — add `Hash` to the derive list.

Success criteria: `cargo check -p thermal-core` passes.
notes: Added Hash to AgentState derive list: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]. cargo check -p thermal-core passes.

title: Fix: thermal-core Rect::grid divide by zero
notes: Added early return guard to Rect::grid(): if cols == 0 || rows == 0 { return vec![]; }. Consistent with split_horizontal/split_vertical which already had this guard. cargo check -p thermal-core passes.

title: Fix: thermal-launch Buffer alloc every frame
notes: DONE. Added cached_query_buf, cached_query, cached_entry_bufs, cached_results, cached_selected fields to LauncherSurface. render_frame() now rebuilds query buffer only when query string changes, entry buffers only when results list or selected index changes. Eliminates ~540 glyphon allocs/sec at 60fps.

title: Fix: thermal-lock shader hardcoded 1080.0
notes: Done: WGSL TimeUniform extended with width/height fields. fs_main divides by vec2(u_time.width, u_time.height). update_time() accepts width/height params. Call site passes self.width, self.height.

title: Fix: dotfiles bootstrap.sh greetd not installed
prompt:
BLOCKER FIX — bootstrap.sh line 18 runs `sudo systemctl enable greetd` but `greetd` is not in packages.txt. With `set -e`, pacman will succeed but `systemctl enable greetd` will fail (unit not found), aborting the entire bootstrap.

Fix: Add greetd and regreet to packages.txt.

File: /home/builder/dotfiles/packages.txt

Find the `# GUI — Lock & Clipboard` section and add before or after it a new section:
```
# GUI — Display Manager
greetd
greetd-regreet
```

Also create the greetd config so it's ready post-install. Create /home/builder/dotfiles/config/greetd/config.toml:
```toml
[terminal]
vt = 1

[default_session]
command = "regreet"
user = "greeter"
```

And update /home/builder/dotfiles/install.sh to symlink it:
- Add: `mkdir -p ~/.config/greetd && ln -sf ~/dotfiles/config/greetd/config.toml ~/.config/greetd/config.toml`
(First read install.sh to see the existing symlink pattern and follow it.)

Success criteria: `grep greetd /home/builder/dotfiles/packages.txt` returns results. bootstrap.sh `set -e` will no longer abort on the systemctl line.
notes: CODEX BLOCKER. bootstrap.sh enables greetd but it's not in packages.txt. Either add greetd+regreet+config or remove the systemctl enable line. set -e will abort bootstrap.

title: Fix: dotfiles missing brightnessctl + wallpaper
prompt:
BLOCKER FIX — Two issues in dotfiles:

1. hypridle.conf lines 10-11 call `brightnessctl -s set 10` and `brightnessctl -r` but brightnessctl is not in packages.txt.
   Fix: Add `brightnessctl` to packages.txt under `# GUI — Wayland / Hyprland` section.

2. hyprland.conf line 13: `exec-once = swww img ~/wallpaper.jpg ...` — this file won't exist on a fresh install, causing swww to fail silently (or error).
   Fix: Change this line to check for the file first, or use a fallback solid color:
   OLD: `exec-once = swww img ~/wallpaper.jpg --transition-type grow --transition-duration 2`
   NEW: `exec-once = test -f ~/wallpaper.jpg && swww img ~/wallpaper.jpg --transition-type grow --transition-duration 2`
   (The `test -f` prevents swww from erroring on missing file. User can drop wallpaper.jpg in ~ later.)

Files to edit:
- /home/builder/dotfiles/packages.txt: add `brightnessctl` under GUI — Wayland / Hyprland
- /home/builder/dotfiles/config/hypr/hyprland.conf: line 13, wrap swww call in test -f check

Success criteria: `grep brightnessctl /home/builder/dotfiles/packages.txt` returns a result. Line 13 of hyprland.conf does not blindly exec swww on a missing file.
notes: CODEX BLOCKER. hypridle calls brightnessctl (not installed). hyprland.conf loads ~/wallpaper.jpg (doesn't exist). Add brightnessctl to packages.txt, ship a default wallpaper or remove the exec line.

title: Fix: dotfiles broken Rofi power menu
notes: CODEX BLOCKER. Waybar power button runs 'rofi -show power-menu -theme thermal-power' but thermal-power.rasi doesn't exist. Create the theme + power menu script, or remove the button.

title: Fix: install guide missing microcode + staged approach
prompt:
DOCUMENTATION FIX — ARCH-INSTALL-GUIDE.md is missing critical information.

File: /home/builder/projects/thermal-desktop/ARCH-INSTALL-GUIDE.md

Read the full file first, then make these additions:

1. In Phase 8 (GPU Drivers section), add CPU microcode installation BEFORE the GPU section:
```markdown
## Phase 7.5: CPU Microcode (IMPORTANT)

Microcode updates fix CPU bugs and security vulnerabilities.

### AMD (Ryzen 5800X)
```bash
pacman -S amd-ucode
```

### Intel
```bash
pacman -S intel-ucode
```

GRUB will auto-detect and load microcode on next boot.
```

2. After the final reboot section, add a staged install approach section:
```markdown
## Staged Install Strategy (Recommended)

Don't install everything at once. Do it in two stages:

### Stage 1: TUI Only (boot into working terminal first)
```bash
bash ~/dotfiles/bootstrap.sh --tui-only
```
Verify: ZSH, Neovim, tmux all work. Fix any issues here.

### Stage 2: Full GUI
```bash
bash ~/dotfiles/bootstrap.sh --gui
```
Verify: Hyprland starts, Waybar shows, Kitty launches.
```

3. Add a post-install verification checklist at the end:
```markdown
## Post-Install Checklist

- [ ] Boot succeeds, GRUB shows Arch + Windows
- [ ] WiFi/ethernet works (`ping archlinux.org`)
- [ ] ZSH is default shell (`echo $SHELL`)
- [ ] Neovim opens (`nvim`)
- [ ] Hyprland starts (`Hyprland` from TTY)
- [ ] Waybar visible
- [ ] Kitty terminal launches (Super+Return)
- [ ] Audio works (`pamixer --get-volume`)
- [ ] GPU acceleration active (`glxinfo | grep renderer` or `vulkaninfo`)
```

Success criteria: File is updated with microcode section, staged install note, and post-install checklist.
notes: CODEX. Add amd-ucode/intel-ucode to install guide. Add staged install approach (TUI first, GUI second). Add post-install verification checklist.

title: Fix: tasks.json duplicate task id 33
prompt:
BUG FIX — /home/builder/tasks.json has two tasks with id=33:
- id 33: 'Build: thermal-conductor (native agent dashboard)'
- id 33: 'Build: thermal-bar (custom status bar)'

Fix: Read the full tasks.json, find the second occurrence of id 33 (the thermal-bar task), and renumber it to 34. Then check if any subsequent tasks use ids that conflict (34, 35, etc.) and renumber those sequentially.

Steps:
1. Read /home/builder/tasks.json
2. Find all tasks, sort by their position in the array
3. Find the duplicate: second task with id=33 is the 'thermal-bar' task
4. Renumber it to max_existing_id + 1
5. Check for any further gaps/conflicts and fix
6. Write the corrected JSON back

The fix must preserve all other fields of every task. Only the `id` field of the duplicate changes.

Success criteria: `python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/home/builder/tasks.json')); ids=[t['id'] for t in d['tasks']]; assert len(ids)==len(set(ids)), 'duplicates found'"` exits with no error.
notes: CODEX. ~/tasks.json has two tasks with id 33. Fix numbering.

title: Fix: dotfiles add polkit agent + rofi-wayland
prompt:
BLOCKER FIX — Two Wayland compatibility issues in dotfiles:

1. No polkit authentication agent. Hyprland needs one for privilege prompts (sudo GUI apps, package managers, etc.) without it, privilege prompts silently fail.
   Fix: Add `polkit-gnome` to packages.txt under `# GUI — Wayland / Hyprland` section.
   Then add to hyprland.conf autostart (after the existing exec-once lines):
   `exec-once = /usr/lib/polkit-gnome/polkit-gnome-authentication-agent-1`

2. packages.txt has `rofi` (line 102) which is the X11 version. On Hyprland/Wayland, this runs through XWayland and can have issues. Replace with `rofi-wayland`.
   Fix in packages.txt: change `rofi` to `rofi-wayland`.
   Note: rofi-wayland is an AUR package, not in official repos. If sticking to pacman only, add a comment noting this limitation:
   `# rofi-wayland  # AUR: use 'yay -S rofi-wayland' after bootstrap`
   And keep `rofi` in packages.txt as the fallback.
   Actually: add `rofi-wayland` as a comment with AUR note, keep `rofi` as the pacman fallback.

Files to edit:
- /home/builder/dotfiles/packages.txt: add polkit-gnome, annotate rofi-wayland
- /home/builder/dotfiles/config/hypr/hyprland.conf: add polkit exec-once after line 15

Success criteria: `grep polkit-gnome /home/builder/dotfiles/packages.txt` returns a result. hyprland.conf contains polkit exec-once line.
notes: CODEX. Add polkit-gnome or similar for privilege prompts on Hyprland. Use rofi-wayland instead of rofi (Xwayland).
